title: coelorachis geminata
intro: Coelorachis geminata, also known as "Two-horned grass", is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its unique two-horned spikelets. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Coelorachis geminata include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Coelorachis geminata are typically made into infusions, decoctions, or poultices, which can be applied topically or consumed as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
